acceptance_criteria: Each major claim in the post is tagged in substance by what supports it: institutional fact, accounting identity, deterministic model, or ABM result.
The household-versus-user-versus-issuer distinction is central to the evidence flow rather than a side note.
The post preserves humility by stating what the simulations illuminate and what they do not prove.
No stale figure or deprecated model claim remains in the narrative.
constraints: Weaken the prose if the simulations land narrower than hoped.
Do not use any model result as universal proof just because it makes the narrative cleaner.
description: After the simulation tasks settle, revise the post so each major section tells the same story: austerity frameworks misassign money's functional role, and that mistake can be illustrated through comparative simulation. Explicitly mark where the evidence is accounting logic, institutional description, ABM behavior, or simplified model behavior.
goal: Integrate the final draft around a clear claim-support structure in which each major point is either conceptual, institutional, or simulation-backed, with the boundary made explicit.
implementation_notes: Integrated the post around the settled simulation surfaces from tasks 018-021. Added the comparative-constraints model, the ABM policy-lab result, and the minimal capacity-boundary model to the narrative; rewrote section 7 so evidence types are explicit; updated reproducibility commands; and synced the audit/spec/handoff so future sessions no longer operate on the pre-repair evidence map.
title: Claim-to-simulation integration pass for money-minsky-policy-mismatch
